column5: Description

column2: ABAP SOAP rfc brute force login
column5: Brute force login using ABAP SOAP rfc interface.

column2: ABAP critical FM calls per SOAP rfc
column5: Client calls critical ABAP function modules per SOAP rfc interface.

column2: ABAP System Ping Failed Health Check
column5: Checks if the ABAP system is reachable via system ping. An alert is raised in case subsequent system ping attempts are failing.

column2: ESP System Ping Failed Health Check
column5: Checks if ESP server is reachable via ping. An alert is raised in case subsequent ESP server ping attempts are failing.

column2: SAP HANA Partitioning unsuccessful Health Check
column5: Checks if SAP HANA partitioning job is working
Checks if pseudonyms generation is working

Category: Unauthorized logon attempt Purpose: Monitor ABAP Security Audit Log event code \"BUS\" indicating a request without sufficient security characteristic of address, e. g. XSRF attacks (note 1619912) Prerequisites: Enable event BUS (note 2073809) Customization Value Lists: 1.  Critical system roles for such a request, e. g. \"Production\" 2. Exclusion lists (allowlists) for: - allowed systems and roles - URLs allowed for a request with weak security

column2: Access to critical database tables via RFC
column5: External Access via RFC to password hash table and other sensitive database tables.

column2: Access to critical database tables via transactions
column5: Access via transaction (e.g. SE16, SE16N, SM30, SM31, SM34, etc.) to password hash table.
Generates an alert when user looks into critical data using client 066.

column2: Alert after hacker logged on from another user
column5: After the user looks into critical database which contains all user password hashes, the attacker might be able to decode some passwords and use the valid credentials to logon into the system.
Alert Security Audit Log event code BUX.

column2: Blocklisted function modules in productive systems
column5: A blocklisted function module has been executed.
A blocklisted report has been executed.
A blocklisted transaction has been executed.
A system has called a blocklisted http url path.
Any activity on client 006 e.g. transaction, program, RFC and HTTP calls leads to an alert

column4: Access to hash table and logon
column5: Alerts a logon attempt after the same network address had access to USR02 of the system

column4: Access to hash table and logon
column5: This Indicator triggers when accessing USR02 (e.g. user password hashes)
Alert when a user is assigned critical profiles (e.g. SAP_ALL or SAP_NEW).
Alert when a user is assigned critical profiles (e.g. SAP_ALL or SAP_NEW) and later logged on successfully
Alert when a user who is not an administrator has changed a role or profile of a user.

column2: Assign user to ADMIN user group
column5: Alert when a user has been assigned to the ADMIN user group.
Alert when an authentication within a role is changed to *.
Alert when a user role has been changed.
Alert a critical authorization (e.g. SAP_ALL or SAP_NEW) assignment per debugging.
Alert when a reference user was assigned to a user.

column2: Assign user to admin group (not ABAP group)
column5: Alert when a user is assigned to one admin user group. Such user groups are not ABAP user group.

column2: J2EE Authorization Assignment to non-admin Role
column5: Alert if a critical authorization is assigned to a role which is not the Administrator role.

column2: Brute force attack and successful logon
column5: The number of failed logons from one terminal ID has exceeded a threshold, and there was a successful logon from this terminal ID.
The number of failed logons from one terminal ID has exceeded a threshold.
User tries to read client independently via debugger
A security relevant configuration has been changed successfully or unsuccessfully, such as audit configuration or security session management.
A system relevant configuration has been changed successfully or unsuccessfully.

column4: Critical RFC Calls For User Management
column5: Category: Unauthorized access to user and access management. Purpose: Monitor critical RFC callbacks for user management. Customization Value Lists: 1. Critical actions for user management 2. Exclusion lists (allowlists) for allowed users and network IPs

column2: Calls between a non-productive and a productive system
column5: A non-productive system has called a productive system.

column2: RFC calls from non-productive to productive systems
column5: Raise an alert when a non-productive system calls via remote function call (RFC) protocol to a productive system
A request without sufficient security features has reached an ABAP server. This could be due to a  CSRF (Cross-site request forgery) token attack.
Access to sensitive data. Every download not performed by program \"ZSWFL_FREE_CUSTOMER_FROM_SWCENT\" and containing values from the \"SuspiciousFilenameDownload\" value list causes an alert.
A user debugs using the new ABAP debugger in SAP GUI.
A user debugs using ABAP Development Tools for SAP NetWeaver (ABAP in Eclipse).

column2: Debugging with change of control flow while debugging
column5: A user changes the control flow while debugging.

column2: Debugging with change of variable values during debugging
column5: A user changes variable values while debugging.

Debugging takes place in a critical system ID. Add the IDs for critical systems into value list CriticalSystemIDsDebugging in order to raise this alert.The format for ABAP based system IDs is <Name>/<Client number>.

column2: Debugging in systems assigned to critical roles
column5: Debugging takes place in a system assigned to a critical role (e.g Production). Add the names of critical system roles into value list CriticalSystemRolesDebugging in order to raise this alert. Enter role values from the system context

column2: Debugging by users belonging to a critical user group
column5: Debugging takes place by a user belonging to a user group which must not debug in a system. Add the names of critical user groups into value list CriticalUserGroupsDebugging in order to raise this alert. For ABAP based systems the user group values can be found in the ABAP system in transaction SU01 in the field 'User Group' in Tab 'Logon Data' using F4-help.
A user debugs using the old ABAP debugger in SAP GUI.

column2: DoS attack against different HTTP URLs
column5: The number HTTP URLs called from one terminal ID have exceeded a threshold (default: 300 different HTTP URLs called per 30 minutes).

column2: DoS attack via RFC_PING/RFCPING to one destination
column5: The number of RFC_PING/RFCPING function calls to a single RFC destination and initiated by one terminal ID has exceeded a threshold (default: 1000 per 30 minutes).

column2: DoS attack against different RFC destinations
column5: The number of RFC destinations that are used from one terminal ID has exceeded a threshold (default: 20 different RFC destinations called within 30 minutes).

column2: DoS attack on sap/public/icf_info URL path
column5: The HTTP URL sap/public/icf_info* has been called. Such URLs could be used to find out server-specific information e.g. URL prefixes which are handled by the ABAP server.

column2: DoS attack from one terminal to different systems
column5: The number Systems called from one terminal ID have exceeded a threshold (default: 10 different Systems called per 10 minutes).

Directory Traversal is a result of poor input validation and is a common attack.  This pattern looks at the Security Audit Log Event Codes to see if directory traversal activities have been detected.  The event codes are listed below: CUQ
column6: Logical file name &A not configured. Physical file name &B cannot be validated. CUR
column7: Physical file name &B does not meet requirements set by logical file name &A. CUS
column8: Logical file name &B is not a valid alias for logical file name &A. CUT
column9: No validation active for logical file name &A. DU5
column10: There is no logical file name for path &A\". During the call of a function for FTP-based file access, no compatible logical file name was found for the check for avoiding directory traversals. The further execution of the function is therefore blocked. Use SAP Notes 1692988 and 1497003 for further information.

column5: Alert dynamic program execution which is suspicious for a code injection attack as indicated by the Security Audit Log event code.

column2: Execution of critical SQL statements with report RSDBA850
column4: Execution of critical SQL statements using SQL console
column5: Monitor critical SQL statements (e. g. DROP TABLE) being executed in the SQL console with report RSDBA850 Prerequisites: Enable read access logging (RAL) for transaction DB12. Customization Value Lists: Critical SQL Executions Exclusion lists (allowlists) for allowed systems and roles
The threshold for unsuccessful logon attempts using RFC/CPIC has been exceeded. The default threshold value is 3.

column2: Failed logon with too many attempts
column5: One or more unsuccessful logon attempts have occurred. The default threshold value is 3.

column2: Failed logon of same user from different Terminal IDs
column5: The number of unsuccessful logons from the same user from different terminal IDs has exceeded a threshold.

column2: Failed Logon with too many password logon attempts
column5: A user account was locked because of too many failed attempts to log on with user ID and password. There are further logon attempts for this user. The number of subsequent logon attempts has exceeded a threshold.
The number attempts to logon with an expired user account has exceeded a threshold.
The number attempts to log on to a user account which has been locked by administrator or due to failed logon attempts has exceeded a threshold.

column2: CUZ-Generic table access by RFC pattern
column5: Category: Unauthorized logon attempt\\nPurpose:\\nMonitor ABAP Security Audit Log event code CUZ indicating generic table access via RFC using functions like RFC_READ_TABLE (note 1539105). \\n Prerequisites: Enable event CUZ (note 2073809) \\n Customization Value Lists: \\n1. Tables not allowed for generic access by RFC (e. g. USR02)  \\n2. Exclusion lists (white lists) for allowed systems and roles \\n

column2: HANA Configuration for Force first password change
column4: HANA Configuration for Force first password change
column5: Default passwords are required to be changed. This pattern monitors settings which enforce change of password for the first usage of an account.
A user has downloaded data from an SAP system exceeding a defined threshold (default: 10.000.000 bytes per download).
A user has downloaded data from an SAP system more times than a defined threshold (default: 10 per day).

column2: Manual Execution of critical Function Module in Trx SE37
column5: This pattern looks at the Read Access Logs (should be preconfigured) to see if a user is executing critical function module in transaction SE37. This could indicate manual execution of critical functionality.

column2: Manual Execution in SE37 with debugging
column5: This pattern looks at the Read Access Logs (should be preconfigured) and Business Transaction Log to see if a user is debugging critical function module in transaction SE37 and is manipulating with its execution in debugger. This could indicate manual execution and manipulation with critical functionality.

column2: Manual Execution in SE37 and critical debugging functions
column5: This pattern looks at the Read Access Logs (should be preconfigured) and Security Audit Log to see if a user is debugging critical function module in transaction SE37 and is manipulating with its execution in debugger. This could indicate manual execution and manipulation with critical functionality.
Critical change of system configuration Config 0001 Every event with event code Q19 causes an alert.
Nonstandard system access
This pattern looks at the Security Audit Log Event Code to see if a Transaction failed to start.  This could indicate an unauthorized use of a transaction.
Unauthorized logon attempt. Dialog logon attempt yields message ID \"DU0\" in Security Audit Log, e. g. message text \"Invalid SAP GUI data\".
Nonstandard system access, RFC function call Security audit log message ID \"DU3\": Server is not contained in the allowlist. SAP security notes: 1605054, 1686247, 2312710

column2: Sensitive Data Download via Blocklisted Reports
column4: Sensitive Data Download via Blocklisted Reports
column5: Access to Sensitive Data Every data download using reports listed in \"ABAPBlocklistedReports\" causes an alert.
An SPNego replay attack has been detected in the system.

column2: Password changed for SAP standard users
column5: The password of an SAP standard user has been modified (for example, DDIC andSAP*, contained in value list SAPStandardUsers delivered by SAP and extensible by customer).

column2: Password changed for SAP standard users and logon
column5: The password of an SAP standard user has been modified and later logged on successfully.

A successful or unsuccessful logon using a password logon method has occurred in a system where the password logon method is not allowed. Prerequisite: The necessary system settings must be applied in the systems. Enter these System IDs of these systems into the value list AllowlistSystemsPasswordLogonForbidden
A successful or unsuccessful logon attempt with SAP standard users has occurred.

A logon by a blocklisted user has succeeded. Prerequisite: Only non-pseudonymized users can be added to the valuelist LogonBlocklistedUsers. A user is not pseudonymized in case its usertype corresponds to one of the usertypes defined in valuelist NonPseudonymizedUserTypes. Another option to avoid pseudonymization is to add usernames to the valuelist SAPStandardUsers. Then maintain the value list SuspiciousLogonBlocklistedUsers with the respective usernames.

column2: Logon success same user from different Terminal IDs
column5: The number of successful logons with the same user from different terminal IDs has exceeded a threshold.
A successful logon to ABAP system client 066 has occurred. For more information about client 066, see http://help.sap.com/saphelp_46c/helpdata/en/2e/d9530294f911d283d40000e829fbbd/content.htm
This pattern looks at the Security Audit Log Event Code to see if a Transaction has been unlocked.  This could indicate an unauthorized use of a transaction.

column2: Password changed multiple times for same user
column5: The password of an SAP user has been modified multiple times. The default threshold is 3 changes to the same user within one day.

The password of a user has been modified by a user who is not an administrator. For ABAP systems, this is identified by user group in SAP User Management, attribute \"User group\" of tab \"Logon Data\" in transaction SU01.
A user has created another user and then logged into the system with this user from the same terminal ID.

column2: User morphing by changing user type
column5: A user type has been changed from a non-dialog to a dialog user.

column2: User morphing by changing user type and logon
column5: A user type has been changed from a non-dialog to a dialog user and this changed user performs a logon within one week.

column2: Attempt to invoke servlet on J2EE

column2: Successfully invoke servlet on J2EE server
column5: A client access to J2EE per servlet successfully. The server responded with 20x code.
Server receives an HTTP request with unexpected methods.
A table has been dropped or altered.
The number of selects from one user and from one terminal ID exceeds a threshold (default: 100000 within 30 minutes).

column2: Generic access to critical database tables
column5: Client gets access to critical database tables.
A call to the service in ICF path /sap/public/info has taken place.

column2: ABAP function modules with removed RFC enablement

This pattern exemplifies the integration between Read Access Logging and SAP Enterprise Threat Detection. You can use this generic RAL pattern as a blueprint to create your own RAL patterns tailored towards the specific user interactions you want SAP Enterprise Threat Detection to monitor in your ABAP system landscape.
The pattern triggers an alert when the audit policy rules in HANA will be disabled
The pattern triggers an alert when the global audit policy in HANA will be disabled

column5: Issue an alert if there are a lot of failed web requests due to security reasons.

column2: Log4J attack attempt via http path
column5: Issue an alert if someone tries to call services using an http path containing the Log4J attack recognition string

column2: Log4J attack attempt via filling of any parameter
column5: Issue an alert if someone enters the Log4J attack recognition string into any fillable parameter

column2: Log4J attack attempt via execution of SQL statements

column2: Successful logon from same Terminal ID with different users
column4: Logon with same Terminal ID with different users
column5: Issue an alert when different users log on successfully from the same terminal ID.

column2: Failed logon from the same Terminal ID with different users
column4: Logon with same Terminal ID with different users
column5: Issue an alert when the number of unsuccessful logons from the same terminal ID from different users has exceeded a threshold.

column2: Low Log Volume per System Group
column5: The pattern is designed to check if the connected System Groups are still sending data above the configured threshold, and it also needs to send at least one log in the specified time range in order to raise an alert. The checked System Groups can be adjusted by configuring the contained value list.

column2: Low Log Volume per Log Type and System Group
column5: The pattern is designed to check for each System Group and Log Type if the amount of logs sent is still above the configured threshold. Also, the System Group and log type needs to send at least one log in the specified time range in order to raise an alert. The checked System Groups and log types can be adjusted by configuring the contained value lists.
Issue an alert when many unsuccessful connect attempts or many VALIDATE attempts have happened.
Issue an alert when authentication or authorization changes have happened.
Issue an alert when critical system configuration changes have happened.
Issue an alert when log backup or recovery activities have happened.
